sections.paragraph:
ἀποκνέω fut. ἀποκνήσω; 1 aor. inf. ἀποκνῆσαι (Ath. R. 65, 20) (s. ὀκνέω; Thu. et al.; Ael. Aristid. 34 p. 664, 9 D. al.; PCairZen 416, 3; PColZen 64, 6; 10 [both III b.c.]; PBad 51, 6) hesitate, have misgivings Ox 654, 22 (ASyn. 256, 54).—DELG s.v. 1 ὄκνος.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

inf. inf. = infinitive

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

PCairZen PCairZen = Zenon Papyri, Catalogue général . . . du Musée du Caire—List 4

PColZen PColZen = Columbia Papyri III, IV: Zenon Papyri—List 4

PBad PBad = Veröffentlichungen aus der badischen Papyrussammlungen—List 4

Ox Ox = POxy, but refers to fragments of documents esp. relating to the lexical data base—List 1

ASyn. ASyn. = KAland, Synopsis Quattuor Evangeliorum13—Lists 1, 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 113
